channel.title: FairPay Payments Foundations Course
channel.description: A six-week audio preparation series for the FairPay Payments Foundations Course. Each episode provides a 15-20 minute overview of the week's content—perfect for commute listening before you dive into the full materials.
channel.itunes:author: FairPay
channel.itunes:summary: A six-week audio preparation series for the FairPay Payments Foundations Course. Each episode provides a 15-20 minute overview of the week's content—perfect for commute listening before you dive into the full materials.

item.title: Week 1: Foundations & Ecosystem Mapping
item.description: Build the mental model every payments decision depends on. We cover clearing vs settlement, finality, push vs pull payments, and map Australia's payments ecosystem—RBA, AP+, AusPayNet, and where FairPay fits.
item.content:encoded:
Before you dive into Week 1, prime your thinking with this audio overview. In 18-20 minutes, you'll understand:

  • Clearing vs Settlement — Why the movement of information isn't the same as the movement of money
  • Finality — When a payment is truly irreversible, and why PayTo's strong finality is both an advantage and a responsibility
  • Push vs Pull — How PayTo combines pull payment mechanics with customer control
  • The Ecosystem — Who the key players are: RBA, AP+, AusPayNet, and the regulatory landscape

Listen on your commute, then dive into the full Week 1 materials.
item.itunes:title: Week 1: Foundations & Ecosystem Mapping
item.itunes:author: FairPay
item.itunes:summary: Build the mental model every payments decision depends on. Clearing vs settlement, finality, push vs pull, and Australia's payments ecosystem.

item.title: Week 2: Rails Selection & Account-to-Account
item.description: Deep dive into NPP, PayTo, and BECS. Learn the 8-criteria framework for rail selection and build your decision matrix for FairPay's use cases.
item.content:encoded:
Week 2 is about making rail selection decisions. This audio prep covers:

  • The 8-Criteria Framework — Availability, data richness, authorization model, reversibility, fraud posture, integration burden, unit economics, access constraints
  • NPP & PayTo Deep Dive — Platform architecture and the agreement lifecycle
  • BECS Reality Check — Why the 2030 end-date was removed and what it means for your strategy
  • Building Your Matrix — How to apply the framework to your specific use cases
item.itunes:title: Week 2: Rails Selection & Account-to-Account
item.itunes:author: FairPay
item.itunes:summary: The 8-criteria framework for rail selection. NPP, PayTo, and BECS deep dive.

item.title: Week 3: Cards & Processing Stack
item.description: Understand card economics (interchange, scheme fees, LCR) and map your payments technology stack with build vs partner decisions.
item.content:encoded:
Cards are your competitive context. This audio prep covers:

  • Card Economics — Interchange + scheme fees + acquirer margin = merchant service fee
  • Least-Cost Routing — How LCR is changing the competitive landscape
  • The Technology Stack — Six layers from frontend to reconciliation
  • Build vs Partner — A framework for deciding what to own and what to outsource
item.itunes:title: Week 3: Cards & Processing Stack
item.itunes:author: FairPay
item.itunes:summary: Card economics and payments technology stack. Build vs partner decisions.

item.title: Week 4: Standards & Regulatory Mapping
item.description: Navigate ISO 20022, EMV, PCI DSS, and Australian regulatory requirements. Map your regulatory perimeter and build a compliance roadmap.
item.content:encoded:
Compliance is an existential concern. This audio prep covers:

  • Technical Standards — ISO 20022, EMV tokenisation, 3-D Secure, PCI DSS v4.0
  • Australian Regulators — RBA, ASIC, AUSTRAC, Treasury licensing reforms
  • Key Dates — Timeline of regulatory milestones through 2026
  • Building Your Roadmap — What's required before launch vs what builds over time
item.itunes:title: Week 4: Standards & Regulatory Mapping
item.itunes:author: FairPay
item.itunes:summary: ISO 20022, PCI DSS, and Australian regulatory requirements. Build your compliance roadmap.

item.title: Week 5: Fraud, Economics & Integration
item.description: Understand Australian fraud landscape, build your risk register, construct unit economics, and design operational workflows.
item.content:encoded:
Making FairPay operationally real. This audio prep covers:

  • Australian Fraud Landscape — $816M CNP fraud and why PayTo is different
  • Risk Register — Categories, scenarios, controls, and KRIs
  • Unit Economics — Cost stack, pricing strategy, sensitivity analysis
  • Operations — Reconciliation, onboarding, support workflows
item.itunes:title: Week 5: Fraud, Economics & Integration
item.itunes:author: FairPay
item.itunes:summary: Fraud landscape, risk register, unit economics, and operational workflows.

item.title: Week 6: Partnerships, Trends & Capstone
item.description: Finalize your partner strategy, track external changes, and complete your FairPay Payments Strategy Pack.
item.content:encoded:
The final week—pulling it all together. This audio prep covers:

  • Partner Categories — NPP access, BECS sponsor, KYC/KYB, fraud tools, compliance advisory
  • Negotiation Priorities — Commercial, risk, data, security
  • External Watchlist — RBA decisions, AUSTRAC reforms, licensing evolution
  • Capstone Overview — What your Strategy Pack should contain and how it's assessed
item.itunes:title: Week 6: Partnerships, Trends & Capstone
item.itunes:author: FairPay
item.itunes:summary: Partner strategy, external changes, and completing your capstone Strategy Pack.
